title: A Psalm of David, for her that obtains the inheritance.

verses.t: Give ear to my words, O Lord, attend to my cry.
Attend to the voice of my supplication, my King and my God: for to thee will I pray, O Lord.
In the morning thou shalt hear my voice; in the morning will I stand before thee, and will look up to thee.
For thou art not a God that desires iniquity; neither shall the worker of wickedness dwell with thee.
The transgressors shall not stand in thy sight: thou hatest, O Lord, all them that work iniquity.
Thou wilt destroy all that speak falsehood: the Lord abhors the bloody and deceitful man.
But I will enter thy house in the multitude of thy mercy: I will worship at thy holy temple in thy fear.
Lead me, O Lord, in thy righteousness because of mine enemies; make my way plain before thy face.
For there is no truth in their mouth; their heart is vain; their throat is an open sepulchre; with their tongues they have used deceit.
Judge them, O God; let them fail in their counsels: cast them out according to the abundance of their ungodliness; for they have provoked thee, O Lord.
And let all that trust in thee be glad in thee: they shall exult for ever, and thou shalt dwell among them; and all that love thy name shall rejoice in thee.
For thou, Lord, wilt bless the righteous: thou hast encompassed us as with a shield of favour.
